# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_servicebustopic (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the topic.
- `namespace` (String) Servicebus namespace name. A namespace is a scoping container for all messaging components. Multipletopics can reside within a single namespace.
- `resource_group` (String) Name of resource group.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_delete_on_idle_in_seconds` (Number) Time idle interval after which a topic is automatically deleted. The minimum duration is 5 minutes.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `default_message_time_to_live_seconds` (Number) Default message timespan to live value. This is the duration after which the message expires, starting from when the message is sent to Service Bus. This is the default value used when TimeToLive is not set on a message itself.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `duplicate_detection_time_in_seconds` (Number) TimeSpan structure that defines the duration of the duplicate detection history.
- `enable_batched_operations` (Boolean) Value that indicates whether server-side batched operations are enabled.
- `enable_express` (Boolean) Value that indicates whether Express Entities are enabled. An express topic holds a message in memory temporarily before writing it to persistent storage.
- `enable_partitioning` (Boolean) A value that indicates whether the topic is to be partitioned across multiple message brokers.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `max_message_size_in_kb` (Number) Maximum size (in KB) of the message payload that can be accepted by the queue. This property is only used in Premium today and default is 1024.
- `max_size_in_mb` (Number) The maximum size of the topic in megabytes, which is the size of memory allocated for the topic.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `requires_duplicate_detection` (Boolean) A value indicating if this topic requires duplicate detection.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Assert the state of the topic. Use `present` to create or update and use `absent` to delete.
- `status` (String) Status of the entity.
- `support_ordering` (Boolean) Value that indicates whether the topic supports ordering.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
